element_introduction_order.mastery_context: core mechanic - player builds initial squad before encountering enemies
high-level troop deployment and movement between territories
turn-based combat with action points, line of sight, cover mechanics
optional stealth movement, exploration of buildings and terrain
progressive acquisition of handguns, rifles, heavy weapons, armor
assigning mercs to tend wounds, repair gear, train local militia
ground and aerial vehicles enable faster sector traversal
mortars, RPGs, LAWs, mines, bombs for destructible environments

element_introduction_order.element: Local militia training and sector defense
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: trainers convert NPCs to militia; guards defend while mercs deployed elsewhere
NPCs only visible when spotted; enemies can interrupt turns with leftover action points

typical_relief_triggers: retreating to safe sector for medical/repair
defeating enemy force and zone becomes secure
typical_tension_triggers: enemy force spotted on tactical screen (switches to turn-based)
low supplies or injured mercs in hostile territory
time pressure from enemy advances on strategic map
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based tactical combat punctuates real-time exploration; strategic map allows player-controlled pacing with optional time acceleration; non-linear sector conquest enables varied tension ebb/flow

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: buildings and rooftops provide optional exploration; stealth mode signals non-mandatory engagement paths

difficulty_curve.early_game: Initial mercenary selection and light enemy contact; player establishes squad composition and economic base through laptop store
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased enemy numbers and variety; militia training becomes strategic necessity; vehicle acquisition enables multi-sector operations
difficulty_curve.late_game: Heavy weapons encounters, superior enemy forces, strategic pressure to consolidate territory and prepare final assault on Deidranna
notable_spikes: first real combat encounter (transition from safe exploration to turn-based danger)
sectors with destructible cover forcing tactical repositioning

notable_level_patterns.description: Game world divided into grid sectors; player captures territories sequentially or non-linearly; each sector contains tactical combat, exploration, and NPC interactions
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Arulco country map (fictional Latin American nation)

notable_level_patterns.description: Exploration in real-time isometric view; upon enemy detection, game shifts to turn-based combat with action point economy per character
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Any tactical engagement within a sector

notable_level_patterns.description: Mercs assigned to non-combat roles (medical triage, equipment repair, skill training) while others conduct operations; creates secondary resource management layer
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Home base sectors designated for supply/training

notable_level_patterns.description: Strategic map controls high-level troop movements and time flow; tactical screen enables granular squad commands and environmental interaction; laptop provides economic/hiring decisions
